title: deserialized DB has a sqlite3_db_filename() contrary to docs
description:
larrybr added on 2021-04-15 09:29:54:
Post #1 in this thread, https://sqlite.org/forum/forumpost/e28b13c50e , details how to demonstrate this misbehavior. (I have not replicated because I can see by reading the code that it behaves just as described.)
Because the incorrect "filename", "x", cannot be an absolute pathname, this might be considered a doc bug, if documented. However, that would be messier than altering the code to conform to the docs. So it's a code bug for now.
drh added on 2022-11-18 21:23:19:
I think the following CLI script demonstrates the problem:

.open --hexdb
| size 4096 pagesize 4096 filename x1.db
| page 1 offset 0
|      0: 53 51 4c 69 74 65 20 66 6f 72 6d 61 74 20 33 00   SQLite format 3.
|     16: 10 00 01 01 00 40 20 20 00 00 00 03 00 00 00 01   .....@  ........
|     32: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 03 00 00 00 00   ................
|     48: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 01 00 00 00 00   ................
|     80: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 03   ................
|     96: 00 2e 66 e8 0d 00 00 00 00 10 00 00 00 00 00 00   ..f.............
| end x1.db
.mode qbox
PRAGMA database_list;

The ".open --hexdb" makes use of sqlite3_deserialize().  Then the
"PRAGMA database_list;" uses sqlite3_db_filename() (or an internal
equivalent) to determine the filename, which you can see comes out
as "x".

┌─────┬────────┬──────┐
│ seq │  name  │ file │
├─────┼────────┼──────┤
│ 0   │ 'main' │ 'x'  │
└─────┴────────┴──────┘
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, name String, value Float64 DEFAULT 0.0);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('test2', NULL);
SELECT id, name, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT count() AS total_rows FROM test_table;
key_bug_features: sqlite3_db_filename() returns incorrect filename after deserialization
